evidence_quotes.quote: developes claim they have the right to remove access for the product for any or no reason.

paragraphs.text: I am concerned on the side of the video game industry, as developers are increasingly deciding to shut down service for videogames already sold to customers. I personally have had many games that I have bought become unplayable due to online functions being deactivated. As it stands developers claim they have the right to remove access for the product for any or no reason. Almost 1 and a half million people already support the 'Stop Destroying Videogames' citizens initiative in the EU so I'm sure many other people share my concerns. I hope for legislation that means developers have to keep games in a reasonably playable state after support ends, or publish software development documentation that allow consumers to create and run their own servers to continue to play live-service games.
